refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements as a traceable baseline with metadata (source, timestamp, derivation method)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail
CR-NGHE-005.4: Calculate and document explicit safety margins for all performance requirements
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite mission parameters (terrain, payload, cycle profile) and applies deterministic derivation rules to compute performance targets; stores baseline snapshots in a versioned configuration database with full audit logging; exposes traceability queries via API.

DP-B: Distributed performance derivation where each vehicle computes its own performance targets locally based on mission context, syncs to a central repository, and maintains local + central history logs.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with system-assisted validation and history tracking; no automatic derivation.
DP-D: Hybrid approach: automatic derivation engine for standard mission profiles; manual override capability for non-standard scenarios; centralized baseline and history management with audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine with deterministic derivation rules, versioned baseline database, and full audit logging. This DP is selected because: (1) it ensures consistency and traceability across all worksite missions; (2) centralized baseline management eliminates synchronization risk and supports audit requirements; (3) deterministic rules are verifiable and safety-critical; (4) audit logging directly satisfies acceptance criteria for maintained baselines/calibration history; (5) it minimizes coupling between vehicle and performance management logic.

potential_sources: Coupling risk: Performance derivation rules must be updated when mission profiles or vehicle capabilities change; requires change control process.
Coupling risk: Worksite mission context inputs must be standardized and validated; requires interface contract with Site Manager/Tasking system.
Coupling risk: Safety margin calculation rules must be reviewed and approved by Safety Manager; requires governance linkage.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via well-defined input contracts (mission context schema), rule versioning, and change control. No fundamental architectural coupling detected. DP-A decouples vehicle logic from performance analytics, reducing diffusion.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured schema (terrain type, payload mass, cycle profile, safety constraints).
ASSUMPTION: Performance derivation rules are deterministic and can be expressed as algorithms or lookup tables.
ASSUMPTION: Baseline database and audit logging infrastructure are available or can be provisioned.
RISK: If mission context is highly variable or unstructured, derivation rules may become brittle; mitigation: design rule engine for extensibility.
RISK: If safety margin rules are not well-defined, audit trail alone does not ensure safety; mitigation: require Safety Manager approval of margin rules before deployment.
RISK: High-frequency updates to performance targets could overwhelm audit logging; mitigation: implement batching and retention policies.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the scope of 'worksite mission context' and define the schema for mission inputs (e.g., terrain classification, payload range, cycle time targets, safety constraints). Establish a formal process for deriving and validating performance margin rules with Safety Manager. Define audit logging retention and query performance requirements.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates all subsystems (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and provides the performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking capability. This module owns the performance analytics engine, baseline database, audit logging, and traceability reporting functions.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle profile, safety constraints) from Site Manager or mission planning system. Used as input to performance derivation rules.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance requirements, baseline snapshots, and history records to monitoring and reporting systems. May also receive actual performance data from Vehicle for comparison against baselines.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and margin requirements from Safety Services; outputs performance requirements with embedded safety margins and alerts if derived targets violate safety policies.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for baseline database synchronization, audit log transmission, and traceability query access across distributed sites.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level module that naturally owns performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit logging. This is a core system function, not a vehicle-specific or teleoperation-specific capability. Containment is very high; minimal diffusion risk.

Vehicle module could theoretically compute performance targets locally, but this would scatter derivation logic across multiple vehicle instances, creating diffusion and consistency risk. Vehicle should consume performance requirements, not derive them. Low containment score reflects poor fit.

Teleoperation module is focused on remote operation and control; performance requirement derivation is orthogonal to tele-operation logic. Placing this function in Teleoperation would create unnecessary coupling and diffusion. Low containment score.

Safety Services could own safety margin calculation and validation, but the broader performance analytics engine (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability derivation) is not a safety-specific function. Safety Services should provide constraints and validation rules, not own the entire derivation engine. Moderate containment score reflects partial fit.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit logging functions are core system-level capabilities that belong in the NGHE Solution module. This module acts as the system integrator and owner of cross-cutting concerns like performance analytics and traceability. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of these requirements, not owners.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution module becomes a monolithic 'god object' owning too many unrelated functions, it could become difficult to maintain and test. Mitigation: decompose NGHE Solution into logical sub-modules (e.g., PerformanceAnalytics, BaselineManager, AuditLogger) at the next decomposition level.

RISK: Performance derivation rules may need to be updated frequently based on field experience; if rules are tightly coupled to NGHE Solution, updates could require system-wide regression testing. Mitigation: design rule engine as a pluggable, versioned component with change control.

RISK: Audit logging could become a bottleneck if high-frequency updates occur; if centralized in NGHE Solution, it could impact system responsiveness. Mitigation: implement asynchronous logging and batching strategies.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: At the next decomposition level, decompose NGHE Solution into logical sub-modules: (1) PerformanceAnalyticsEngine (owns derivation rules and algorithms), (2) BaselineManager (owns baseline versioning and storage), (3) AuditLogger (owns audit trail recording and querying), (4) TraceabilityReporter (owns traceability reporting). This will reduce monolithic risk and improve maintainability. Clarify the interface contract between NGHE Solution and Safety Services for safety margin rule governance.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (centralized performance analytics engine with versioned baseline database and audit logging) directly addresses all aspects of the refined FR: automatic derivation from mission context, baseline recording with traceability, managed history with audit trail, and safety margin documentation. The DP is specific, verifiable, and feasible. No fundamental mismatch detected.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): If performance derivation rules are not properly encapsulated, they could be duplicated or scattered across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: enforce rule engine as a single, centralized component within NGHE Solution.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): Audit logging could be implemented locally in multiple modules instead of centrally. Mitigation: establish audit logging as a shared service within NGHE Solution with a well-defined API.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct module boundary for this DP. Containment score of 95 indicates very high fit. The module owns the system-level performance analytics and traceability functions. External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and appropriate for a system-level module. No containment issues detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Automated tests can validate derivation rules against known mission contexts and expected performance targets; (2) Baseline snapshots can be inspected for completeness and metadata; (3) Audit logs can be queried to confirm all updates are recorded with timestamp, source, and approver; (4) Traceability reports can be generated and manually reviewed for completeness. No fundamental verification barriers detected.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit logging. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are consumers of these requirements. Interfaces are well-defined (Tasking in, Telemetry out, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link inout). Ownership boundaries are unambiguous.

recommended_revisions: Refine the schema for worksite mission context inputs (Tasking interface) to ensure all necessary parameters for performance derivation are captured.
Define the performance derivation rule set and safety margin calculation algorithms in detail; document these as part of the DP specification.
Establish a formal change control process for performance derivation rules, including Safety Manager review and approval.
Define audit logging retention policies, query performance requirements, and access control rules.
At the next decomposition level, decompose NGHE Solution into logical sub-modules to avoid monolithic design.

decision.justification: The refined FR is well-defined and addresses the acceptance criteria. The selected DP is sound and feasible. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution) is the correct module boundary with high containment score (95) and minimal diffusion risk. All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass. However, the FR is not fully atomic; it contains five sub-FRs that require further decomposition to reach atomic requirements. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to detail the performance derivation rules, baseline management logic, audit logging mechanisms, and traceability reporting functions. This will enable more precise DP selection and IM assignment at lower levels.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements as a traceable baseline with metadata (source, timestamp, derivation method)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail
CR-NGHE-005.4: Calculate and document explicit safety margins for all performance requirements
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history

assumptions: Worksite mission context can be captured in a structured schema (terrain type, payload mass, cycle profile, safety constraints).
Performance derivation rules are deterministic and can be expressed as algorithms or lookup tables.
Baseline database and audit logging infrastructure are available or can be provisioned.
Safety margin rules are well-defined and can be formally specified.
Worksite mission inputs are provided via the Tasking interface with sufficient detail for performance derivation.
Safety Services module provides safety constraints and margin requirements via SafetyAlert interface.
open_risks: RISK: If mission context is highly variable or unstructured, derivation rules may become brittle. Mitigation: design rule engine for extensibility and validation.
RISK: If safety margin rules are not well-defined, audit trail alone does not ensure safety. Mitigation: require Safety Manager approval of margin rules before deployment.
RISK: High-frequency updates to performance targets could overwhelm audit logging. Mitigation: implement batching and retention policies.
RISK: NGHE Solution module could become monolithic if too many functions are assigned to it. Mitigation: decompose into logical sub-modules at next level.
RISK: Performance derivation rules may need frequent updates based on field experience; tight coupling could impact maintainability. Mitigation: design rule engine as pluggable, versioned component.
evidence_needed: Detailed specification of worksite mission context schema (inputs to performance derivation).
Formal definition of performance derivation algorithms and rules (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability calculations).
Safety margin calculation methodology and approval from Safety Manager.
Audit logging requirements (retention, query performance, access control).
Baseline database schema and versioning strategy.
Traceability reporting requirements and query examples.
Change control process for performance derivation rules.
Interface contracts for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link.
